So, coming back to the discussion, the first part, what are the three types of storms which
we can encounter in our lives?
Mind you when I am talking about storms, difficulties, challenges, as we go on in the session, you
will see that these storms are irrespective of caste, color, creed, religion, rich man,
poor man, black, white, Indian, American, anybody will have to encounter these kind
of challenges.
Our scriptures have classified the challenges we encounter in life, or the storms which
we will have to face, broadly speaking in three categories.
The first category is called Adhyatmik kleshas.
Kleshas means difficulties, challenges, Adhyatmik kleshas, which broadly means miseries arising
from our body and mind, Adhyatmik kleshas.
And the second category is Adhibhautik kleshas, miseries due to other living entities.
Adhyatmik kleshas.
Other living entities means other living beings.
It could be animals, it could be other human beings.
And the third category is Adhidevik kleshas, miseries due to natural calamities.
The super cyclone which Kolkata had to brace a few days back, Amphan, would come and destroy
it.
It would come under that category.
Miseries due to natural calamities are the Devik kleshas.
So we will go one by one, before we get into the discussion, why these calamities land
up in our lives, why we have to encounter these challenges, first we will understand
what these categories are.
And then we will discuss.
And then we will get into the solution part of it.
The first thing I mentioned was Adhyatmik kleshas due to our body and mind.
This was way back in 2003 or maybe 2005.
There was a very eminent gentleman, very powerful person.
His name was Nanak Dev.
He was a very powerful man.
He was a very good man.
He was a very good man.
He was a very good man.
position. He was working in the, he was part of a finance committee at the national level
which was chaired by the finance minister of the country. So there was a committee of about
10 to 15 people and he was part of that committee, the eminent committee which would make
you know the financial, the financial planning, the budgets, allocation of resources,
so being part of the committee obviously he was in touch with the who's who of the country.
He would just, he could have just picked up a phone and called Mr. Ratan Tata or could be
anybody, any big industrialist or any eminent person of the country. So he visited our kitchen
in Bangalore, Akshaya Patra had just started, it was in 2001-2002
and
he was based out of Delhi. He was very impressed with this initiative
and helped us in many ways. Today Akshaya Patra is a very big brand but in those days
it was relatively unknown, people were not aware of this initiative
and he would call many eminent people and somehow get them connected or we would fix up some
appointment so that we could go and give a presentation, tell them,
about what we are doing and many of them got connected with Akshaya Patra and started
supporting Akshaya Patra and then a few years passed somehow we lost touch with this gentleman
and in around 2010 a vice chairman gets a call from him and in a very feeble
voice he says, Swamiji how are you? Long time we haven't met. Nice talking to you over the phone.
He called up, how is Akshaya Patra doing, how many children are you feeding these days.
He was inquiring and then towards the end of the call he said, Swamiji when you are coming to Delhi
please find time to visit me.
Come back.
to my place it will be my privilege to have you with us so yes yes why not we will surely come
we will definitely meet we are privileged to come to your place and spend some time
and then few days later
a vice chairman happened to visit delhi and went to his place
and this gentleman comes the bell rang gentleman comes up personally to open the door and take
swamiji inside and from his looks he could make out that he was a completely different man with
what we had seen him in 2005 2003 he was completely different
completely frail, weak, barely able to walk. He had a cane and then he made Swamiji sit
on the sofa in his drawing room and then started off with some discussion and at a certain
point he held the hands, both hands of Swamiji and said, Mami ji, you are very close to God.
You all are spiritual people. You are spending your time. He was aware of our daily schedule.
He had visited our temple also. You all spend a lot of time in chanting prayers, chanting
mantras, doing meditation. So you are very close to God. I have a request. Please pray
to God that He very soon takes me away. And there was some kind of tears in his eyes.
Enough. I've seen enough of life. Now it's too much, Swami. I just want to die. So Swamiji
held his hand and said, no, no, why are you saying like that? Because he was also caught
unaware. Rich, eminent person, part of that finance committee, deciding the budget for
the country.
Allocating resources, dealing with hundreds and thousands of crores. Maybe regularly meeting
the finance minister of the country. That person is saying, I've seen enough. I just
want to leave. Pray to God that I leave this. Swamiji became a little curious. He says,
why are you saying so?
He said, for the last 4 years, 5 years, I have a severe backache. I can't sit and sleep.
I can't walk. Even now, when I am sitting, talking to you, there is so much of pain.
Name a hospital. It's his name of country he traveled. It is his name of country. So
you know different countries because he was very eminent person rich person
he tried you know taking different kinds of treatment different best of hospitals in the
world in the new york singapore best of the hospitals he had gone consulted the best of doctors
and eventually because the pain was so unbearable he was hii was to kill that give that temporary
relief he was given some prescription involves some painkillers and because the pain was so
intense he had even done surgery where the search written although back surgery is quite risky not
very much recommended but still as a技术

Last resort he had to undergo a surgery and still the pain was so unbearable that he was prescribed some painkillers and because he had to take 8 to 10 painkillers in a day at different times of the day, his body, there was a reaction, ulcers, blisters had got formed.

He couldn't eat properly, there were ulcers in his stomach, he couldn't digest the food properly, could not sleep, could not sit, could not walk and he was just, you know, pleading, pray to God that I should just keep this, you are close to God, he will hear your prayers.
Now mind you, this adhyatmic kleshas which I mentioned about.

It is not that only poor people, now here we have migrant labourers, you know, they are Shramik Express going and we hear day in day out that they don't have food to eat and they had to travel in this kind of conditions, walking barefoot.
We can understand, sometimes we may even feel that because, you know, poverty, unfortunately they had to go through all this.
Maybe rich people are exempted, yes, maybe they are exempted from one kind of adhyatmic kleshas.
But we have examples of even rich people succumbing to diseases like cancer, so many other diseases.
So it is not that only poor people, only people from third world countries, now we have, you know, the US, just two days back I was reading an article,
they have 5,000 people have succumbed to this Coronavirus pandemic.
With such advanced medical facilities, people are by and large very rich.
There is lot of opulence, there is lot of technology.
Still people are falling sick, people are getting infected and people are dying.
So, adhyatmikta, miseries due to our body and mind.
So, this is the first category.
As I mentioned, everyone will encounter this.
Everyone will encounter this.
We may have a sense that because I have a policy, because I am rich, because I am quite strong, because I go to gym daily, because I have, you know, I am quite physically fit.

I am very conscious about my diet, just a question of time, just a question of time, body will become frail, weak, vision will become blurred, our legs will start wobbling and eventually we have to leave.
Adhyatmik kleshas.
Then we have adhyatmik kleshas.
Adhyatmik kleshas.
Adhyatmik kleshas.

Although yes, some precautions is being taken, some prevention is being planned but then by and large helpless.

the material world, we will have to encounter Adibhautik kleshas, Adhyatmik kleshas, Adidevik

Now the second part which we want to discuss today is why these calamity.
On one side we say God is all-powerful, God is our loving father, he has created this
world.
So one possibility is that things have gone out of his control.
He doesn't want his children to suffer but unfortunately what to do, there is another
powerful force which is much more powerful than him, maybe because of which he is held
helpless.
You know at times in a family the father, he wants his child, wants good for his child
but at times he is helpless.
In front of his eyes he can see his child suffering.
So one possibility is that maybe there is another powerful force, maybe a demoniac force
which is much more powerful and Lord who is our loving father, who is kind, who is very
kind.
He is also helpless.
The answer is no, it is not that the Lord is helpless, the other part of it then somebody
may say if he is not helpless, if he is all-powerful, maybe he is not very compassionate, he is not
very merciful, he is not very loving.
Otherwise why do we see so much of suffering in this world?
So much of misery.
So many lakhs and lakhs of people dying, either he is not all-powerful or if you say that
he is all-powerful, maybe he is not very merciful, he is a sadist when he sees that his children
or his people all around in his creation are suffering, maybe he enjoys, he gets some kind
of a rasa, some kind of a pleasure.
So there are people who are called sadists who get some kind of a pleasure when they
see suffering of others.
The answer is our scriptures very forcefully, very emphatically say that the Lord is our
supreme father, the loving father, we are his part and parcel.
Krishna also declares in the Gita, you are my amshas, you are my part and parcels.
But in the Gita it says, .
Prakrtisthanikarshati because of the mind and the senses, mana shashta indriyani because

when it comes to dealing with the nature and the nature retaliates and then we try to advance
technologically come up with research and come up with some vaccines, come up with some
medical devices, some medicines which will push back some of the diseases which tormented
us, tormented humanity, we have pushed back but nature comes back with another kind of
a disease, another kind of a force.
When hurricanes come, even recently when there was forest fires, the whole country Australia
was helpless, they couldn't do much, just wait and watch for the fires to subside.
When it was very, very difficult, when it was very, very difficult, when it was very,

very close to Canberra you know they just to protect the city, there was two, three

Imagine, capital, and the fires are raging, approaching the city.
So, if we are saying that Lord is all powerful and He is very merciful then why these calamities?
It looks some kind of a contradiction. And because people don't have an answer, many people become an atheist or they lose faith in God.
There is no God. It's all in the mind. It's all blind belief. It's all superstitious.
And that's how there are people who are very well-read, very knowledgeable so-called scholars.
They become atheists. They write books on why God doesn't exist.
We have to get the right understanding. God does exist. He is the creator.
He is sarva-karana-karanam. He is the cause of all causes.
He is all-powerful. He is omnipotent. He is omniscient. He knows everything.
And He is all-kind. He is all-merciful.
It is possible that all these qualities can be there in that Supreme Lord person
and still understand why these calamities are happening in this world.
We have come in this material world, which is the material world.
We have come in this material world, which is the material world.
This material world is also called bhava-sagra. Ocean of birth and death. Ocean of material existence.
Bhava-sagra. Sagra means ocean. Bhava-rog. Material existence.

Parastasmatu bhavanyo vyakta-avyakta-sanatana.
This material world is vyakta and avyakta. It is manifest and unmanifest.
It is manifest and unmanifest.
But beyond this material world, there is parastasmatu bhavanyo vyakta-avyakta-sanatana.
It is sanatana. It is eternal.
In that world there is no misery. There is no pain.
There is no adhyatmik, adibhautik and adidevik kleshas.

what is the use of topics
which simply waste one's valuable life?
A life is wasted in topics
not related with the Lord.
What should I do?
As I mentioned,
while we are in this material world,
we have to engage in some activities
which may not be necessarily
fully Krishna conscious.
We will have to pursue some goals
which are not Krishna conscious goals
so long as we are in this material world.
As I mentioned earlier,
while we are engaged in those activities,
we cannot bring those activities to nil.
We can always aspire for,
we can pray to the Lord,
we can beg the Lord.
If not in this life,
next life we will get an opportunity.
But while we are in this material world,
we should try to be Krishna conscious.
When Chaitanya Mahaprabhu toured South India,
one person wanted to renounce everything
and come with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu instructed him
sthane sthite shruti gatam
to be where you are.
sthane sthite shruti gatam
but hear the messages of God.
So yes,
we will not be able to put complete end
to our material activities
but we can,
with the help of Krishna conscious,
transform those activities
and bring, as far as possible,
Krishna into picture.
This question is from Uma Kumar.
When materialists see us suffering physically,
they are surprised.
They have the understanding that Lord protect us.
How to explain to them?
We have discussed in one of the sessions,
Lord's protection does not mean that
physically no harm will come to a devotee.
Lord's protection does not mean that
a devotee will not have any physical ailment.
Lord's protection does not mean that
a devotee will not have COVID infection.
We have to have a much better understanding
of what is Lord's protection
and then we will not be bewildered
when even so-called devotees
appear to be suffering.
I gave an example of, you know,
a cat carrying a kitten in the mouth.
It appears that the kitten is experiencing death.
So we will have to discuss that more elaborately
and maybe in one of the sessions we will talk.
So this comes to an end, the session.
It's already 6-2.
Thank you very much for joining us.
It was nice to have all of you
for today's Gita Live discussion.
Look forward to having you next Sunday also,
same time, 4.30 pm.
Hare Krishna.
